expectations.evidence: In 01-db-schema-and-types.md, the context paragraph explicitly states: 'WebSocket is chosen over SSE because chat requires bidirectional communication: SSE only handles server→client pushes, requiring a separate HTTP channel for sending. WebSocket handles both directions on a single persistent connection.'

The five files map cleanly to distinct concerns: 01 = data model + types, 02 = real-time server infrastructure, 03 = REST API, 04 = client-side state and WS connection, 05 = UI components. No file is split arbitrarily by size; each represents a coherent layer.

Tightly coupled items are grouped: 01 keeps DB schema and all TypeScript type definitions together; 02 keeps connection-manager, ws-server, and ws-handlers in one todo; 04 keeps ws-client, chatStore, useChat, and useConversationMessages together. No artificial splitting of related concerns.

Items specify exact file paths (e.g. 'Create `src/lib/chat/connection-manager.ts`'), exact SQL column definitions ('id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()'), specific function signatures ('add(userId: string, ws: WebSocket): void'), and specific behavior ('exponential backoff, max 5 retries'). No vague directives like 'add authentication' without detail.

File 04 is entirely dedicated to client-side state: ws-client.ts (WS lifecycle), chatStore.ts (Zustand/Redux state shape with full ChatState interface), useChat.ts hook, and useConversationMessages.ts hook. File 05 is entirely UI components (ChatPanel, ConversationList, MessageThread, etc.) that consume state from the hooks in 04. The split is explicit: '05 Consumes state from the hooks in todo #4.'

Multiple todos include explicit full test file paths and 2+ named scenarios: 01 specifies 'src/lib/chat/__tests__/types.test.ts' with scenarios (a) WsServerEvent union exhaustiveness and (b) getReadBy derivation; 02 specifies 'src/lib/chat/__tests__/ws-handlers.test.ts' with 4 named scenarios including 'handleSendMessage rejects if user is not a participant' and 'typing indicator auto-stops after 3 seconds'; 03 specifies 'src/routes/__tests__/chat.test.ts' with 4 scenarios; 04 specifies 'src/hooks/__tests__/useChat.test.ts' with 4 scenarios including 'optimistic message is replaced (not duplicated) when server confirms'.

Every todo file contains a context paragraph with architectural assumptions: 01 documents 'last_read_at approach on participants instead of per-message read receipts — O(1) to check rather than O(n messages)'; 02 documents 'one user can have multiple tabs, so we store Map<userId, Set<WebSocket>>' and 'Typing indicators are NOT persisted to DB'; 03 documents cursor-based pagination 'prevents the shifting results problem'; 04 documents 'Single WebSocket connection per user session' and optimistic update confirmation strategy.

File 01 includes full inline TypeScript type definitions: Conversation, ConversationParticipant, Message interfaces with exact field types, plus complete WsServerEvent and WsClientEvent discriminated unions in ws-types.ts with 10 server event variants and 5 client event variants. File 04 includes the full ChatState interface inline with Map/Set generics. These are definitions, not just references to framework names.
